steps.label: Member Demographics and MCI Search
steps.description: On this screen, you will enter the member's demographic information, such as name, date of birth, and contact details. You will also be able to search for existing records in the Master Client Index (MCI) to avoid duplicate entries. Please review the instructions and ensure all required fields are completed accurately. After entering the information, use the 'Search MCI' button to check for existing records. If a match is found, you can link to the existing record; otherwise, you will proceed to create a new one. Guidance and field-level help are provided to assist you in this process.

fields.label: SSN
fields.error_message: SSN must be in valid format.

fields.label: Email Address
fields.error_message: Email must be a valid email address.

data_validation: Date of Birth cannot be in the future
SSN must be unique if provided
Phone number must be in valid format
Email must be in valid email format
If member is under 18, guardian information must be collected in subsequent steps

buttons.label: Search MCI
buttons.description: Initiates a search in the Master Client Index using the entered demographic information.

buttons.label: Clear
buttons.description: Clears all entered fields to allow re-entry of information.

actions.name: Validate and Trigger MCI Search
actions.implementation: Validates all required demographic fields, including unique SSN, valid date of birth (not in future), and required guardian info for minors. If validation passes, initiates a search in the Master Client Index using the entered demographic information and transitions to the MCI Search Rule step. If validation fails, displays appropriate error messages for missing or invalid fields and prevents search until resolved.
actions.triggerCondition: On 'Search MCI' button click and after successful validation.

actions.name: Handle Demographic Validation Error
actions.implementation: If demographic validation fails, displays specific error messages for each invalid or missing field. Provides options to edit information and retry validation. Highlights fields with errors and allows the user to correct them without clearing all data.
actions.triggerCondition: Validation fails on 'Search MCI' button click.

actions.name: Edit Member Information After Validation Error
actions.implementation: Allows the Intake Specialist to edit any demographic field after a validation error is displayed. Changes are re-validated on subsequent 'Search MCI' attempts.
actions.triggerCondition: On user editing any demographic field after validation error.

actions.name: Clear Member Information
actions.implementation: Clears all entered member demographic fields on the screen and resets validation errors.
actions.triggerCondition: On 'Clear' button click.

steps.label: Search MCI Record
steps.description: This rule governs the process of searching for an existing member record in the Master Client Index (MCI) system using the demographic information entered by the Intake Specialist. Upon clicking the 'Search MCI' button, the system initiates a query to the MCI database using the provided demographic data. The system returns the match status and, if a record is found, the corresponding record details.

rules.label: Initiate MCI Search
rules.logic: When the Intake Specialist triggers the search, the system uses the entered demographic information to query the MCI. The system must handle any errors returned, such as database or transaction errors, and provide appropriate feedback to the user.

rules.label: Return Search Results
rules.logic: If the search is successful, the system returns the match status and record details. If an error occurs (e.g., transaction closed), the system returns an error message indicating the issue.

actions.name: Handle Successful MCI Search
actions.implementation: If the MCI search completes successfully, evaluates the result: if a matching record is found, transitions to the decision step (STEP_3) to determine next action; if no record is found, also transitions to STEP_3 for further processing. Passes search result data to the next step.
actions.triggerCondition: On successful completion of MCI search (regardless of match).

actions.name: Handle MCI Search Error
actions.implementation: If the MCI search returns an error, displays the error message to the Intake Specialist. Provides options to retry the search, edit demographic information, or escalate the issue to an administrator if repeated failures occur.
actions.triggerCondition: MCI search result status is error.

steps.label: MCI Record Found?
steps.description: This rule defines the decision logic for determining whether a matching MCI record exists for the member based on the results of the MCI search. The system evaluates the search response to check for the presence of a valid record and sets the match status accordingly.

rules.label: Evaluate Search Outcome
rules.logic: The system checks the search result status. If the status indicates success and a record is found, proceed to use the existing MCI record. If the status indicates an error or no record found, proceed to create a new MCI record or display the error message.

actions.name: Proceed to Use Existing MCI Record
actions.implementation: If a matching MCI record is found, triggers the process to link intake to the existing record and transitions to the linkage step (STEP_4).
actions.triggerCondition: Search result status is success and record is found.

actions.name: Proceed to Create New MCI Record
actions.implementation: If no matching MCI record is found, triggers the process to create a new MCI record and transitions to the creation step (STEP_5).
actions.triggerCondition: Search result status is success and no record found.

actions.name: Handle MCI Search Decision Error
actions.implementation: If the search result status is error, displays the error message to the Intake Specialist. Provides options to retry the search or edit demographic information.
actions.triggerCondition: Search result status is error.

steps.label: Use Existing MCI Record
steps.description: This rule specifies the process for linking the intake to an existing MCI record when a match is found. The system populates available demographic fields from the found MCI record and associates the intake with the correct member record.

rules.label: Link Intake to Existing Record
rules.logic: If a matching MCI record is found and the status is successful, the system links the intake to the existing MCI record and populates demographic fields from the record. If an error is present, display the error message and do not proceed with linking.

actions.name: Link Intake to Existing MCI Record
actions.implementation: Links the intake to the existing MCI record, populates all available demographic fields from the found record, and confirms linkage to the Intake Specialist. If linkage is successful, transitions to the next step (STEP_6).
actions.triggerCondition: Successful linkage to existing MCI record.

actions.name: Handle Linkage Error
actions.implementation: If an error occurs while linking intake to the existing MCI record, displays the error message to the Intake Specialist. Provides options to retry linkage or return to demographic entry for editing.
actions.triggerCondition: Error during linkage to existing MCI record.

steps.label: Create New MCI Record
steps.description: This rule governs the process for creating a new member record in the MCI system when no existing record is found. The system uses the entered demographic information to generate a new MCI record and returns the new record ID to the Intake Specialist.

rules.label: Create New Record
rules.logic: If no matching MCI record is found or an error occurs, the system uses the entered demographic information to create a new MCI record. If the creation is successful, return the new record ID. If an error occurs, display the error message.

actions.name: Create New MCI Record
actions.implementation: Uses the entered demographic information to create a new MCI record in the system. If creation is successful, returns the new record ID to the Intake Specialist and transitions to the next step (STEP_6). If an error occurs, displays the error message and provides options to retry or edit demographic information.
actions.triggerCondition: No matching MCI record found and demographic information is valid.

actions.name: Handle MCI Record Creation Error
actions.implementation: If an error occurs during creation of the new MCI record, displays the error message to the Intake Specialist. Provides options to retry creation or return to demographic entry for editing.
actions.triggerCondition: Error during new MCI record creation.

steps.label: Program Eligibility and Case Information
steps.description: This screen allows you to enter detailed information about the member's program eligibility, service needs, and case history. Please review each section carefully and provide as much detail as possible to ensure accurate assessment and service planning. You may add multiple service needs or case history entries as required. Instructions and field-level guidance are provided to help you complete each section. Once all information is entered, you will be able to submit the intake for processing.

fields.label: Service End Date
fields.error_message: Service End Date must be after Service Start Date.

fields.label: Case End Date
fields.error_message: Case End Date must be after Case Start Date.

data_validation: Eligibility Determination Date cannot be in the future
Service End Date must be after Service Start Date
Case End Date must be after Case Start Date
Cannot have duplicate Service Type entries for the same date range
If Case Outcome is 'Closed', Case End Date is required

buttons.label: Submit Intake
buttons.description: Validates and submits all entered program and case information, linking it to the member's MCI record.

buttons.label: Add Service Need
buttons.description: Adds another service need entry for the member.

buttons.label: Add Case History
buttons.description: Adds another case history entry for the member.

buttons.label: Back
buttons.description: Returns to the previous screen to review or edit member demographic information.

actions.name: Validate and Submit Intake
actions.implementation: Validates all entered program eligibility, service needs, and case history information, including checks for eligibility date, service/case date ranges, duplicate service types, and required guardian info for minors. If validation passes, submits the intake, links it to the member's MCI record, and triggers notification scheduling. If validation fails, displays error messages for missing or invalid fields and prevents submission until resolved.
actions.triggerCondition: On 'Submit Intake' button click and after successful validation.

actions.name: Handle Intake Validation Error
actions.implementation: If validation fails during intake submission, displays specific error messages for each invalid or missing field. Provides options to edit information and retry validation. Highlights fields with errors and allows the user to correct them without clearing all data.
actions.triggerCondition: Validation fails on 'Submit Intake' button click.

actions.name: Edit Intake Information After Validation Error
actions.implementation: Allows the Intake Specialist to edit any program eligibility, service needs, or case history field after a validation error is displayed. Changes are re-validated on subsequent 'Submit Intake' attempts.
actions.triggerCondition: On user editing any intake field after validation error.

actions.name: Add Service Need Entry
actions.implementation: Adds another service need entry for the member in the intake form.
actions.triggerCondition: On 'Add Service Need' button click.

actions.name: Add Case History Entry
actions.implementation: Adds another case history entry for the member in the intake form.
actions.triggerCondition: On 'Add Case History' button click.

actions.name: Navigate Back to Demographics
actions.implementation: Returns to the previous screen to review or edit member demographic information.
actions.triggerCondition: On 'Back' button click.

steps.label: Submit Intake
steps.description: This rule defines the process for finalizing and submitting the collected member information. Upon clicking 'Submit Intake', the system validates all intake data, saves the information, links the intake to the appropriate MCI record, and triggers notification scheduling. Any errors encountered during submission are returned to the Intake Specialist.

rules.label: Validate and Save Intake
rules.logic: On submission, the system validates all intake data. If validation passes and the transaction is successful, the intake is saved and linked to the MCI record. If an error occurs (e.g., transaction closed), the system returns the error message and does not complete the submission.

rules.label: Trigger Notification Scheduling
rules.logic: After successful intake submission, the system initiates the scheduling of daily client notifications. If an error is encountered, notification scheduling is not triggered and the error message is displayed.

actions.name: Trigger Daily Client Notification Scheduling
actions.implementation: After successful intake submission and MCI linkage, initiates scheduling of daily notifications to the client at 2am. If notification scheduling is successful, transitions to the notification scheduling step (STEP_8).
actions.triggerCondition: Successful intake submission and MCI record linkage.

actions.name: Handle Intake Submission Error
actions.implementation: If an error occurs during intake submission or MCI linkage, displays the error message to the Intake Specialist. Provides options to retry submission or review and edit entered information.
actions.triggerCondition: Error during intake submission or MCI linkage.

steps.label: Schedule Daily Client Notification
steps.description: Process to automatically schedule daily notifications to the client at 2am after intake submission. The system sets up a recurring job that sends a notification to the client every day at the scheduled time, ensuring timely communication and engagement.

metadata.trigger_condition: Successful intake submission and MCI linkage
metadata.business_logic: Upon successful intake submission and linkage to an MCI record, the system schedules a recurring notification job. At 2am daily, the job triggers and sends a notification to the client using the mapped template. The system tracks the status of each notification and handles errors such as transaction scope issues.

actions.name: Schedule Daily Client Notification and Audit
actions.implementation: Schedules a recurring notification job to send messages to the client every day at 2am using the mapped template. Tracks notification status (Sent, Delivered, Failed) and logs all notification events for audit. If scheduling or sending fails, triggers error handling actions.
actions.triggerCondition: Triggered by successful intake submission and MCI linkage.

actions.name: Handle Notification Scheduling Error
actions.implementation: If an error occurs during notification scheduling or delivery, logs the error and displays the error message to the Intake Specialist. Provides options to retry or notify support.
actions.triggerCondition: Error during notification scheduling or delivery.

actions.name: Audit Notification Events
actions.implementation: Logs all notification scheduling and delivery events, including status (Sent, Delivered, Failed) and error details, for audit purposes. Ensures audit trail is maintained for compliance.
actions.triggerCondition: On notification event (success or error).
